name: Benny Fields
abstract: Benny Fields, occasionally billed as "Bennie Fields" (born Benjamin Geisenfeld; June 14, 1894 – August 16, 1959), was a popular singer of the early 20th century, best known as one-half of the Blossom Seeley-Benny Fields vaudeville team.

concerts.label: Paul Whiteman and his Greater Concert Orchestra
Benefit: Haym Solomon Home for the Aged and Infirm
Benefit: U.S. Convalescent Hospital at Camp Upton, L.I.

about_quotes.speaker: Dorothy Donegan
about_quotes.evidence: Yes, and Benny Fields and Blossom singing. I was in a show called “Star Time.” ... So I learned about show business from them, the vaudeville stuff.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dorothy Donegan — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Lopez
about_quotes.evidence: And we had some entertainers on there. Benny Fields, Benny Davis, Jack Salisbury, Charlie Thorpe. And I had the band.

about_quotes.speaker: George "Jug" Jernberg
about_quotes.evidence: And Benny Fields and Benny Davis and Jack Salisbury were a singing trio in the Windcliff Inn.
